label.text: The Hierarchy
• Workspace: A project file containing global variables and multiple scenes. • Scene: A single node graph (e.g., a specific conversation or level logic).
Saving and Loading
You can save the entire Workspace to keep all scenes together, or export individual scenes as standalone JSON files.
Field Types
Nodes support various data types for dialogue logic:
String / Big Text
Short IDs vs long dialogue blocks.
Number / Bool
For flags, counters, or math logic.
Connects this field to another node.
References Workspace variables.
Right-click a node to edit these fields in detail.
Edge Data
Unlike standard flowcharts, Bark edges can hold their own data. This is useful for 'Choice' text or 'Requirements' (e.g., if a player has an item).
Ports & Connections
Drag from an output port to an input port. Ports can be 'Fixed' or dynamically added via the node inspector.
JSON Structure
When you export a Workspace, the editor creates a folder containing:
1. workspace.json (Global variables and metadata) 2. /scenes (One JSON file per node graph)
This relational structure makes it easy for game engines to load only the scenes they need.
